heading: Special administrative review process
label: 6515 Special administrative review process
notes: Forest Service Pre-Decisional Objection Process, , , provided that:Pub. L. 113–79, title VIII, § 8006(b)128 Stat. 913Feb. 7, 2014“Section 428 of division E of the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2012 ( note; ) shall not apply to any project or activity implementing a land and resource management plan developed under section 6 of the Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Planning Act of 1974 () that is categorically excluded from documentation in an environmental assessment or an environmental impact statement under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).”16 U.S.C. 6515Public Law 112–7416 U.S.C. 160442 U.S.C. 4321, , , provided that:Pub. L. 112–74, div. E, title IV, § 428125 Stat. 1046Dec. 23, 2011“Hereafter, upon issuance of final regulations, the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service, shall apply section 105(a) of the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 (), providing for a pre-decisional objection process, to proposed actions of the Forest Service concerning projects and activities implementing land and resource management plans developed under the Forest and Rangeland Renewable Resources Planning Act of 1974 ( et seq.), and documented with a Record of Decision or Decision Notice, in lieu of subsections (c), (d), and (e) of ([former] note), providing for an administrative appeal process: , That if the Chief of the Forest Service determines an emergency situation exists for which immediate implementation of a proposed action is necessary, the proposed action shall not be subject to the pre-decisional objection process, and implementation shall begin immediately after the Forest Service gives notice of the final decision for the proposed action: , That this section shall not apply to an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project under title I of the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 ( et seq.) children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 30 days after , the Secretary of Agriculture shall promulgate interim final regulations to establish a predecisional administrative review process for the period described in paragraph (2) that will serve as the sole means by which a person can seek administrative review regarding an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project on Forest Service land.December 3, 2003

children.label: A
children.text: beginning after the completion of the environmental assessment or environmental impact statement; and

children.label: B
children.text: ending not later than the date of the issuance of the final decision approving the project.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 3 Eligibility
children.text: To be eligible to participate in the administrative review process for an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project under paragraph (1), a person shall submit to the Secretary, during scoping or the public comment period for the draft environmental analysis for the project, specific written comments that relate to the proposed action.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: The interim final regulations promulgated under paragraph (1) shall take effect on the date of promulgation of the regulations.

children.heading: Final regulations
children.label: b Final regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate final regulations to establish the process described in subsection (a)(1) after the interim final regulations have been published and reasonable time has been provided for public comment.

children.label: A
children.text: the administrative review process established by the Secretary of Agriculture under this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: the administrative hearings and appeals procedures established by the Department of the Interior.

children.heading: Issues
children.label: 2 Issues
children.text: An issue may be considered in the judicial review of an action under only if the issue was raised in an administrative review process described in paragraph (1).section 6516 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An exception to the requirement of exhausting the administrative review process before seeking judicial review shall be available if a Federal court finds that the futility or inadequacy exception applies to a specific plaintiff or claim.

children.heading: Information
children.label: B Information
children.text: If an agency fails or is unable to make information timely available during the administrative review process, a court should evaluate whether the administrative review process was inadequate for claims or issues to which the information is material.
